Nutrition, life coach, this guy, that guy, you know, I frustrated them all, by the way, I didn't buy anything. But but the process, see, it's an older, it's an older means of selling, right? Because otherwise, every one of them, people be trying to get somebody in to meet with them. And most of them would have a tough time. So they've got a feeder system, if you will, to expose prospects to eight things that it would be very difficult to expose prospects to, to eight things on their own. Another piece of leverage. Sales mastery. Again, I think more important than ever. So sales master is more important than ever, okay, because prospects and customers are harder to come by and more expensive to come by. So where slop was affordable before, or slops affordable, if all you want to do is make six figures. So like, the entire time that we were working with Miracle Era, that top my head's coming off, because of the sales process. So somebody makes an appointment, gets in their car, comes to a place in order to have their hearing tested. They go through eight different tests, they're there for an hour and a half. Fairly high level of commitment, right? They're closing 20% of their sales. How can this be? And surely we're going to fix this. Really, before we fix anything else. No, we're not. No, no, no, no, no, no. We're going to do better ads. And we're going to run more people through that we're only going to close 20% of. There's something wrong with this. Now, different environments have different sales closing rates, right? So like selling from the front of the room, books, tapes to a big audience, the percentage by the best is very low. The environment doesn't lend itself to a high closing rate, the quality of the customer, the fact that they come in clumps, and they all share stuff, all sorts of reasons. So like in the in the Peter Low business, that environment, none of us even talk about percentages. We don't even have that conversation. We talk about dollars per head. And so if Zig and Brian and Tommy and I are comparing how well we're doing, we're talking about dollars per head. And we're talking about fairly small dollars. 25,000 people in the room.
